name: Cosmic birefringence anomaly
summary: Re-analysis of Planck 2018 polarization data (Minami-Komatsu 2020) found evidence for a uniform rotation of CMB polarization by beta = 0.35 +/- 0.14 degrees, excluding zero at 99.2% C.L. (later updated to 2.4 sigma with full WMAP+Planck data in 2022). Such a rotation - 'cosmic birefringence' - would violate parity and is naturally produced by ultralight axion-like-particle dark matter or dark energy coupling to the photon field via the Chern-Simons term. If confirmed, the first non-gravitational signature of axion-like physics.

characteristic_numbers.note: Excludes 0 at 99.2% C.L. (2.4 sigma) after marginalizing over instrument-calibration angle via dust-cross-correlation method.
Combined WMAP+Planck analysis (Eskilt-Komatsu 2022, arXiv:2205.13962) reaches 3.6 sigma. Updated 2023+ analyses sustain the signal at ~3 sigma.

principles_stressed.note: Uniform polarization rotation requires P (parity) violation in cosmology - either explicit (Chern-Simons coupling F F-dual) or spontaneous via axion-like-particle background. If confirmed, the strongest hint of cosmological CPT-related new physics.

history: Theoretical framework: Carroll, Field, Jackiw (1990), 'Limits on a Lorentz and parity violating modification of electrodynamics', PRD 41, 1231. Minami-Komatsu (2020), 'New extraction of the cosmic birefringence from the Planck 2018 polarization data', PRL 125, 221301, arXiv:2011.11254. Eskilt-Komatsu (2022), PRD 106, 063503, arXiv:2205.13962. Active follow-up with ACT, SPT, LiteBIRD planned for definitive measurement.

references.citation: Minami and Komatsu, 'New extraction of the cosmic birefringence from the Planck 2018 polarization data', Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 221301 (2020)
Carroll, Field, Jackiw, 'Limits on a Lorentz and parity violating modification of electrodynamics', Phys. Rev. D 41, 1231 (1990)
